resources.string:
• On first launch, WorkCycle guides you through the basic cycle setup.
• Choose a template if one already fits your work schedule.
• If you use a custom cycle, set the start date and choose which label applies on that date.
• Configure cycle rules such as skipped weekends or holidays if needed.
• After setup, cycle and template settings can be changed from Settings.
• Add the Work Cycle or Work Time widget to your home screen if you want quick access.

• The start date is the reference point of the cycle.
• The selected first label applies to that date.
• Each valid workday advances the cycle.
• Skipped weekends or holidays do not advance the cycle.
• Templates may lock some settings when the schedule is predefined.
• The Week, Month and widgets all use the same resolved schedule data.

• Secondary labels add extra work information to a day, such as routes, work areas, shifts or assignments.
• Status labels are separate and cover states such as vacation, sick leave, field work or standby.
• Tap a day in Week or Month to edit its secondary label or status.
• Built-in status labels are translated automatically.
• Custom labels are kept exactly as you entered them.
• You can control secondary label visibility from Settings.

• Week shows an operational overview of the selected week.
• Use week navigation to check nearby days.
• Each day can show the primary cycle, secondary label and status.
• Week is no longer used for cycle configuration.
• To change templates, cycle labels, start date or rules, open Settings.

• Month shows a monthly overview of your schedule.
• Each day can show the primary cycle label, secondary label and status icon.
• Tap a day to see or edit details.
• Month uses the same resolved schedule rules as Week and widgets.
• Short labels are recommended for better readability.

• The Work Cycle widget shows the current schedule directly on your home screen.
• Upcoming days use the same resolved schedule data as the app.
• The Work Time widget shows your current work tracking state and balance.
• Widgets update automatically, but Android may delay updates depending on battery and launcher behavior.
• You can customize widget appearance from Settings.

• Keep primary cycle labels short, such as A, B, Morning, Afternoon or Night.
• Use secondary labels for routes, locations, vacation, sick leave or special duties.
• If the schedule looks wrong, check the template, start date, first label and skipped-day rules.
• Manual Work Log corrections are marked so edited events stay traceable.
• If you enter a manual Work Log time in the future, WorkCycle shows an extra warning.
• In the current version, a finished Work Log day does not show Start again, because one completed session per day is supported.

• Improved first-run setup with clearer onboarding steps
• Fixed startup flow so onboarding appears before What’s New on fresh installs
• Cleaned up Week so it is focused on the operational weekly overview
• Moved legacy setup migration into a safer startup path
• Improved Help text and widget terminology
• Added debug-only developer tools for faster onboarding and local reset testing
• Added technical cleanup and documentation foundations for future v3.0 features
